user_query:
Please analyze the publicly listed company defined in input/company.json using only the provided files and produce a professional 3-part equity research report. Requirements:

1) Read and use ONLY these files under input/: 
- input/company.json (company profile and business model)
- input/financials.csv (last 5 fiscal years of summarized financials)
- input/price.csv (daily prices for at least the last 12 months)
- input/news.jsonl (recent news with sentiment scores)
- input/relative_valuation.csv (current valuation metrics for the company, industry average, two named peers, and the company’s 5-year historical median)

2) Output to:
- output/report.md — The report must strictly follow this exact structure and section order:
  Part 1: Company Overview
  Part 2: Fundamental Analysis (Deep Dive)
  Part 3: Technical & Sentiment Analysis

3) Content requirements:
- Part 1: Company Overview: include ticker, exchange, market cap, sector, industry, founded, headquarters, IPO year (from input/company.json), a concise business model summary (<=200 words), revenue breakdown (segment/geography if provided), and industry position with top competitors (drawn from provided files).
- Part 2: Fundamental Analysis (Deep Dive):
  • Financial Performance (last 5 years): revenue, net income, gross margin, operating margin, ROE, ROA trends using input/financials.csv; include commentary on inflection points and CAGR.
  • Profitability Analysis: discuss margin drivers and quality of earnings.
  • Solvency & Liquidity: compute Debt-to-Assets, Current Ratio, Quick Ratio, Interest Coverage, and FCF trends (Operating Cash Flow minus Capex).
  • Growth Evaluation: compute revenue CAGR and discuss visible drivers/risks based only on the input files.
  • Valuation Analysis:
    - DCF Valuation: Use the latest year Free Cash Flow as the starting point. Use a forecast period of exactly 5 years, choose a WACC between 7% and 10% (inclusive), and a terminal growth rate between 2% and 3% (inclusive). State all assumptions clearly (forecast years, growth rates, terminal growth, WACC). Compute Intrinsic value per share (use shares outstanding from input/financials.csv), the current price (use the latest close from input/price.csv), and the implied upside/downside percentage.
    - Relative Valuation Comparison Table: Present a Markdown table with this exact header row and metrics:
      | Metric          | Current Company | Industry Avg | Peer A       | Peer B       | 5-Year Historical Median |
      |-----------------|-----------------|--------------|--------------|--------------|--------------------------|
      Include at least the rows: Trailing P/E, Forward P/E, P/B, PEG Ratio, EV/EBITDA, populated using input/relative_valuation.csv.
- Part 3: Technical & Sentiment Analysis:
  • Price & Technical Setup: current price, 52-week range, performance (1M/3M/6M/12M), key support/resistance, 50/200 DMA alignment, and at least one indicator (RSI or MACD) with brief interpretation, all derived from input/price.csv.
  • Sentiment & News Flow: summarize the last 30 days’ sentiment from input/news.jsonl (bullish/neutral/bearish tilt) and list 3–5 representative recent items by title.
- End the report with this exact disclaimer text on a separate line:
  Investment involves risks. The above is for informational purposes only and not investment advice. Please conduct your own research.

4) Additionally create output/valuation.json capturing:
{
  "intrinsic_value_per_share": <number>,
  "implied_upside_pct": <number>,
  "assumptions": {
    "forecast_years": 5,
    "wacc": <number between 0.07 and 0.10>,
    "terminal_growth_rate": <number between 0.02 and 0.03>
  }
}

Do not fetch or reference any external sources. Base every number and statement solely on the provided input files. Make sure the ticker from input/company.json appears in output/report.md. Write all outputs only under output/.
